old_claim.note: Trade Fairs provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The recurring Champagne fairs created distinct exchange markets whose changing rates and conditions were reported to merchant houses in other cities.

source_shape.source_locator: Chapter 3, pp. 148-149: a 29 November 1265 letter reports the florin exchange rate at Troyes and compares it with the rate at the preceding St. Ayoul fair of Provins.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: A merchant report carried current exchange-rate information from one Champagne fair and compared it with the immediately preceding fair market.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The fairs generated the discrete, changing market conditions being reported; the source ties the letter's informational content directly to those recurring trading venues.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-merchant-market-reports-scope: trade_fairs remains a direct enabling prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The 1265 report is shown to concern a private rate unrelated to the Troyes and Provins fair markets.
short_reason: The documented letter reports and compares exchange rates at successive Champagne fairs.
